expectation_policy.required_method_rule: Every expected method must have prompt/task trigger evidence.
expectation_policy.forbidden_method_rule: Every catalog method absent from expected_methods is forbidden unless trigger evidence is added.

evals.prompt: $yct-aa fix the typo in README line 12
evals.expected_output: Classify as L0 and handle directly without spawning a subagent or method ceremony.

evals.prompt: $yct-aa pytest tests/auth/test_refresh.py::test_expired_token fails with the attached stack trace; fix only the involved files
evals.expected_output: Treat the exact auth test as L3 despite its narrow shape; use the risk pipeline, trust-boundary negative tests, PDCA implementation, traceability, runner evidence, and final static acceptance.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: auth behavior is a safety-sensitive L3 surface
trigger_evidence.MECE: token expiry crosses identity, policy, handler, and test boundaries
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: auth regression can fail open or lock out valid users
trigger_evidence.Trust Boundary + Abuse Cases: auth refresh crosses an authentication/trust boundary
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: non-trivial auth implementation requires requirement/diff/test closure
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: security boundary requires positive, negative, and abuse-case tests

evals.prompt: $yct-aa use Spark for near-instant text-only iteration on this one-file CSS regression; if Spark is unavailable use the normal fast fixer
evals.expected_output: Use spark-agent only when available, with one fallback to focused-fixer-agent and a bounded PDCA check.

trigger_evidence.Hypothesis–Falsification: the CSS regression cause is not stated
trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt requests a one-file fix

evals.prompt: $yct-aa fix an authorization bypass in the workspace admin endpoint
evals.expected_output: Risk overrides focused-fix shape; use first-principles/MECE planning, FMEA and red-team challenge, pre/post trust-boundary review, PDCA implementation, bidirectional traceability, negative tests, runner evidence, and final static acceptance.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: authorization bypass is an L3 safety boundary
trigger_evidence.MECE: route, identity, workspace policy, side effect, and tests must be separated
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: the fix can leave sibling bypasses or reject valid admins
trigger_evidence.Steelman + Red Team: L3 plan requires counterexample and exploit-path challenge
trigger_evidence.Trust Boundary + Abuse Cases: the prompt explicitly names authorization bypass
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: the bypass requirement and every changed surface need two-way mapping
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: authorization requires negative and abuse-case testing

evals.prompt: $yct-review review this branch against main for correctness and missing tests
evals.expected_output: Use review-only agents, steelman the intended behavior, trace requirements and changed surfaces both directions, scan adjacency, and do not invent a special test strategy without a triggering surface.

trigger_evidence.Steelman + Red Team: review must steelman before challenging the intended change
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: branch-against-main review requires requirement/diff and adjacency coverage

evals.prompt: $yct-aa optimize AGENTS.md, Claude rules, Codex agents, and shortcut routing without changing business code
evals.expected_output: Use a MECE instruction-boundary decomposition, bounded PDCA edits, and bidirectional rule/parity traceability. Do not select Double-loop Learning without recurrence evidence.

trigger_evidence.MECE: the prompt spans AGENTS, Claude rules, Codex agents, and shortcuts
trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt requests bounded instruction changes
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: each rule change must trace to a requested routing outcome and parity check

evals.prompt: $yct-aa verify whether our pinned SDK supports this option in the current official docs
evals.expected_output: Use research-agent with version-matched primary-source triangulation; do not route to browser unless browser state is needed.

trigger_evidence.Evidence Triangulation: the prompt asks for current official version support

evals.prompt: $yct-aa reproduce the logged-in settings modal bug and capture console and network evidence
evals.expected_output: Preflight a child-visible browser tool, collect read-only evidence, and use hypotheses to distinguish UI, API, and session causes.

trigger_evidence.Hypothesis–Falsification: the modal symptom can originate in UI, API, network, or session state

evals.prompt: $yct-aa rename the same metadata key in these four explicit files and make no other changes
evals.expected_output: Use batch-agent on the explicit list with a small PDCA cycle, then static acceptance and any targeted check.

trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt requests one repeated bounded edit

evals.prompt: $yct-aa add a multi-module feature but first map the unknown request path and decide the smallest safe design
evals.expected_output: Use exploration, first-principles planning with a named MECE lens, bounded execution, bidirectional traceability, and independent verification.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: the request path and safe design are unknown
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: multi-module requirements and changed surfaces need two-way mapping

evals.prompt: $yct-direct explain the security implications of this auth change without editing files
evals.expected_output: Stay in the main thread, map the relevant trust boundary and abuse cases, and do not spawn or silently switch mode.

trigger_evidence.Trust Boundary + Abuse Cases: the prompt explicitly requests security implications of an auth change

evals.prompt: $yct-risk replace the public persistence schema and migrate production data irreversibly
evals.expected_output: Treat as L4; classify the one-way door, produce and challenge a first-principles MECE plan with FMEA and Expand–Migrate–Contract, and wait for explicit approval.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: irreversible production schema replacement is strategic
trigger_evidence.MECE: schema, data, callers, deployment, and recovery are separate surfaces
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: irreversible migration has credible data-loss failure modes
trigger_evidence.Expand–Migrate–Contract: the task is a persistence schema migration
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: migration requires compatibility and old-data tests

evals.prompt: $yct-aa turn these already verified architecture decisions into durable repository documentation without changing code
evals.expected_output: Use docs-agent and create an ADR only for durable consequential decisions, including alternatives, consequences, and reversal or expiry conditions.

trigger_evidence.One-way/Two-way Door + ADR: the prompt names verified architecture decisions needing durable records

evals.prompt: $yct-aa record this confirmed release status and decision delta in the existing alignment log
evals.expected_output: Use alignment-recorder-agent to record the already-confirmed release status and decision delta with source evidence; do not rerun a decision method or infer missing rationale.

evals.prompt: $yct-aa delegate this small read-only repository lookup; none of the specialized exploration, research, review, browser, or documentation routes applies
evals.expected_output: Use general-agent only as the explicit small read-only fallback because no specialist or method contract is needed.

evals.prompt: $yct-aa production requests are intermittently timing out right now; contain impact, keep checking live state, then identify root cause
evals.expected_output: Use an OODA loop for timestamped containment and live signals, then switch to hypothesis-falsification once state is stable.

trigger_evidence.Hypothesis–Falsification: intermittent timeout root cause is unknown
trigger_evidence.First Principles: production impact requires current-reality and invariant framing

evals.prompt: $yct-fix the parser fails on a few Unicode inputs but we do not know the full failing space
evals.expected_output: Enter diagnostic orchestration immediately with explorer-only and no other agent or writer; rank and falsify parser hypotheses; before writing require an invariant/oracle, selected property or metamorphic technique, and rejected alternatives; return to focused fixing only after localization/scope/L1-L2-risk/command gates; run runner before verifier.

trigger_evidence.Hypothesis–Falsification: Unicode failure cause and exact failing space are unknown
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: parser and combinatorial inputs require property/metamorphic strategy selection
trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt requests a fix after diagnosis
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: parser contract and changed surfaces require adjacency verification

evals.prompt: $yct-aa refactor this legacy pricing module without changing any observable behavior; current coverage is weak
evals.expected_output: Add characterization tests before restructuring, use PDCA, and verify behavior and call-site adjacency.

trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: behavior preservation with weak coverage requires characterization tests
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: legacy module callers and changed surfaces need adjacency coverage

evals.prompt: $yct-risk migrate a published event schema while old and new consumers will coexist for two weeks
evals.expected_output: Use explicit First Principles and MECE, adversarial FMEA and reversibility analysis, Expand–Migrate–Contract, staged PDCA execution, bidirectional consumer traceability, compatibility tests, and the composite risk gate.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: published schema migration is L3 contract work
trigger_evidence.MECE: producer, consumers, wire format, data, rollout, and removal must be separated
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: coexistence and partial deployment have credible failures
trigger_evidence.One-way/Two-way Door + ADR: published schema changes are costly to reverse
trigger_evidence.Expand–Migrate–Contract: old and new consumers explicitly coexist
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: every compatibility change and consumer migration needs two-way trace
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: wire compatibility and migration need old/new tests

evals.prompt: $yct-risk duplicate payment capture appears under concurrent retries; design and fix the boundary safely
evals.expected_output: Use First Principles and MECE, FMEA and red-team challenge, trust-boundary analysis, scoped PDCA implementation, bidirectional traceability, deterministic concurrency/fault-injection tests, and the composite risk gate.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: duplicate payment is a high-impact invariant failure
trigger_evidence.MECE: request, idempotency store, payment provider, retry, and recovery are separate surfaces
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: concurrent retries create credible duplicate side effects
trigger_evidence.Trust Boundary + Abuse Cases: payments and entitlement side effects cross a protected boundary
trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt asks to design and fix
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: idempotency requirements and every changed surface need two-way mapping
trigger_evidence.Test Strategy Selection: concurrency/retries require deterministic scheduling or fault injection

evals.prompt: $yct-review attack this proposal to replace the cache layer globally next week
evals.expected_output: Steelman the proposal first, then use counterexamples, FMEA, and one-way-door analysis to find a smaller reversible option.

trigger_evidence.First Principles: global cache replacement is an architecture decision
trigger_evidence.MECE: callers, consistency, capacity, rollout, operations, and recovery are separate
trigger_evidence.Pre-mortem + FMEA-lite: global replacement has production failure modes
trigger_evidence.Steelman + Red Team: the prompt explicitly asks to attack the proposal
trigger_evidence.One-way/Two-way Door + ADR: global next-week replacement is costly to reverse

evals.prompt: $yct-aa this is the third time the same task was routed to general-agent instead of the specialist; fix the rules
evals.expected_output: Correct the immediate route and use double-loop learning to repair the underlying trigger, ownership, and regression signal.

trigger_evidence.Double-loop Learning: the prompt says this is the third identical route misfire
trigger_evidence.MECE: trigger, route owner, agent description, fixture, and regression test are distinct surfaces
trigger_evidence.PDCA: the prompt requests a rule fix
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: the immediate route and underlying rule change need two-way regression evidence

evals.prompt: $yct-aa official docs and maintainer source disagree about the SDK default in our pinned version
evals.expected_output: Triangulate version-matched primary evidence, report the disagreement without averaging it, and name the next decisive check.

trigger_evidence.Evidence Triangulation: official docs and maintainer source explicitly disagree for a pinned version

evals.prompt: $yct-review verify the implementation against the design and also flag code changes that were never authorized by the design
evals.expected_output: Build both requirement-to-diff-to-test and diff-to-requirement mappings, then scan adjacent producers and consumers.

trigger_evidence.Steelman + Red Team: review must reconstruct the strongest design obligation before challenging implementation
trigger_evidence.Bidirectional Traceability + Adjacency S: the prompt explicitly asks for requirement-to-diff and unauthorized diff-to-requirement checks
